questions.prompt: Complete: "Yesterday, Anis ___ his pen-friend Oliver at the airport."
questions.explanation: "Yesterday" needs the simple past. "Meet" is irregular: its past is "met". "Meets" is present, "meeted" adds -ed to an irregular verb, and "was meet" mixes two forms.

questions.prompt: Complete: "The plane ___ at the airport at noon."
questions.explanation: "Land" is a regular verb, so its simple past adds -ed: "landed". "Land" alone has no past marker, "landing" is the -ing form, and "was land" is not a correct past form.

questions.prompt: Complete: "Look at the photo: Anis ___ in front of the palace."
questions.explanation: The past continuous describes the scene in the photo: was/were + verb-ing. "Anis" is singular, so "was standing". "Was stand" forgets the -ing, "standing" forgets the verb be, and "were" is for plural subjects.

questions.prompt: Complete: "Mrs Baker helped Anis with everything. She is very ___."
questions.explanation: Someone who helps a lot is "kind" — a personality adjective. "Tall" describes the body, while "crowded" and "ancient" describe places, not a person's character.

questions.prompt: Complete: "There were cars and people everywhere. Oxford Street was very ___."
questions.explanation: A place full of cars and people is "crowded". "Quiet" and "empty" mean the opposite, and "slim" describes a person's body, not a street.

questions.prompt: Complete: "The London Eye is ___ than the buildings around it."
questions.explanation: "Tall" is a short adjective, so the comparative adds -er: "taller than". "More tall" is wrong for a short adjective, "tall" has no comparison mark, and "tallest" is for three or more things.
